sections.paragraph:
παραφυάς, άδος, ἡ (παραφύω ‘produce at the side’, s. prec. entry; Hippocr., Aristot. et al.; LXX; En 26:1) someth. growing off the side, offshoot, side growth.

ⓐ (Theophr., HP 2, 2, 4; Nicander, Fgm. 80 π. of a palm tree; Philo, Plant. 4) Hs 8, 1, 17f; 8, 2, 1f; 8, 3, 7; 8, 4, 6; 8, 5, 2; 5f.

ⓑ in imagery (Aristot., EN 1, 4 [1096] al.; 4 Macc 1:28), of sectarians who, as side growths of a plant created by God, can bear nothing but death-dealing fruit ITr 11:1.—DELG s.v. φύομαι.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

someth. someth. = something

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Nicander Nicander , III/II b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 772
